notes: Drawn one-per-turn during the Shadow phase. The room presses on John mentally — sanity-drain effects model the 24-hour-diner-of-the-soul wear. Spec: lore/_TAROT_GAUNTLET.md §IV.7.

cards.title: THE CLOCK HOLDS
cards.flavor: Three forty-seven AM. Three forty-seven AM. Three forty-seven AM. The hand doesn't move and you notice you weren't watching it move either.

cards.title: THE FLUORESCENT TICK
cards.flavor: Every two seconds, a yellow tick. You start counting between ticks and then forget what you were counting.

cards.title: THE COUNTER WANTS WIPING
cards.flavor: It's almost the same spot. It always is.

cards.title: THE GRAFFITI HAND
cards.flavor: FRANKLY / FOOLISH, in someone's same patient hand.

cards.title: THE BELL RINGS (ONE TONE)
cards.flavor: Brass on coil. One tone. The other waits.

cards.title: THE PAINTED POSE
cards.flavor: Somewhere across town, in a room you've never been to, you are already a painting. You can almost see the brushstrokes from inside your own face.

cards.title: THE STEAMBOAT ECHO
cards.flavor: Red, reflected. In the diner window. Always.

cards.title: THE FELLOW TRAVELER
cards.flavor: Faith's nose finds your ankle. She shakes off something wet.

cards.title: TWELVE YEARS
cards.flavor: The apron knows the date even when you don't.

cards.title: NINE LOCUST SEVENTEEN DOOR
cards.flavor: The number-sequence whispering from under the formica. You realize you've been mouthing it for who knows how long.

effects.note: every subsequent turn standing on COUNTER adds +1 Inertia

cards.title: THE GRILL SMELLS LIKE A FUNERAL
cards.flavor: Onions and old grease and, underneath, a flower-shop smell that has no business being in here. The grill starts to smoke.

cards.title: THE BATHROOM DOOR SWINGS
cards.flavor: It wasn't closed all the way. Now it's open. Now it isn't. The smell rolls out either way.

cards.title: THE SWARM
cards.flavor: Something gathers under the buzzing light over BOOTH 6. They aren't moths.

cards.title: YOUR NAME, IN SOMEONE ELSE'S VOICE
cards.flavor: Soft, from the booth behind you. The exact wrong pronunciation — the one only your sister ever used.

cards.title: THE WINDOW SHOWS THE WRONG RIVER
cards.flavor: It runs upstream tonight and you are the only one in here who can see it doing it. The glass starts to fog from outside.

cards.title: THE SONG YOU'VE BEEN HUMMING
cards.flavor: You can't place it. You've been humming it for hours. The jukebox is unplugged.

cards.title: THE APRON POCKET
cards.flavor: Your hand goes in and finds a note in your own handwriting. You don't remember writing it. You don't read it.
